rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical reaction of 1,4-dicyanobenzene with aryl iodides and alkenes (e.g., styrenes) via radical intermediates generated under Zn anode / RVC cathode conditions in CH3CN with BnNMe3Cl electrolyte.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction generates aryl radical from aryl iodide (and/or radical anion of 1,4-dicyanobenzene); Zn serves as sacrificial anode

key_intermediates.name: benzyl/alkyl C-radical adduct after radical addition to alkene

electron_flow: Cathode reduces aryl iodide (assisted by mediator/radical anion of 1,4-dicyanobenzene) to generate an aryl (phenyl) radical, which adds to the alkene; the Zn anode is oxidized as the sacrificial counter-electrode.

probe_reagents.name: (1-(2-phenylcyclopropyl)vinyl)benzene (8)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 9 isolated in 56%, consistent with radical addition followed by cyclopropane ring opening

probe_reagents.name: DMPO
probe_reagents.observation: EPR spectrum of DMPO adduct observed (g = 2.0072–2.0073, A_N = 14.40 G, A_H = 20.90 G); HRMS m/z 190.12202 matches DMPO–phenyl adduct C12H16NO (calcd 190.12264), supporting phenyl radical

notes: Radical clock with phenylcyclopropyl vinyl substrate gives ring-opened diene product, confirming a carbon-centered radical intermediate. EPR/DMPO trapping with and without added 4-methylstyrene gives the same signal, and HRMS identifies the trapped species as a phenyl radical derived from iodobenzene.
